questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a virtual function in C++?
options: It can be used to create a copy of an object
It can be overridden by derived classes
It can only be used in the base class
It can only be used in the derived class

questions.question: If a class contains a virtual function, does it inherit the virtual attribute?
Which of the following is an example of a pure virtual function in C++?
questions.hint: A pure virtual function in C++ is declared using the = 0 syntax
